title: All Saints' Sunday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## November 7, 2021 | All Saints' Sunday Sermon

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

All Saints' Sunday falls on the first Sunday following November 1st, making November 7, 2021 the primary observance. The RCL readings for this date would be Revelation 7:9-17 (the great multitude before God's throne), 1 John 3:1-3 (beloved children of God), Matthew 5:1-12 (the Beatitudes), with Psalm 34:1-10, 22 as the appointed psalm. The church calendar places this firmly in Ordinary Time following Pentecost, though All Saints' observance shifts focus toward the communion of saints and the witness of faithful departed.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating the post-pandemic reopening period with varying approaches to mask mandates and in-person worship across dioceses. The Diocese of Colorado was part of the Mountain West region with Bishop Robert Wright overseeing diocesan operations; no major episcopal elections or significant controversies were breaking at this specific moment. General Convention had last met in 2018, with the next General Convention scheduled for July 2022 in Baltimore, so the church was in a planning/interim phase.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical late autumn weather with temperature fluctuations between 40-60 degrees Fahrenheit. The Colorado legislature was not in session (the 2022 session would begin in January), but local municipal governance and school board issues were ongoing, particularly surrounding COVID-19 protocols in schools, which remained contentious in Colorado's public discourse. Downtown Denver and the Capitol Hill neighborhood where Saint John's Cathedral is located were seeing gradual return to fuller commercial activity as pandemic restrictions continued easing.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Kyle Rittenhouse trial was dominating national news, with closing arguments occurring on November 3-4, 2021, and jury deliberations continuing through this week (verdict came November 10). The Biden administration was pursuing its infrastructure bill passage and Build Back Better legislation through Congress. The U.S. was seven months into the administration with focus on pandemic recovery, inflation concerns beginning to rise, and midterm election cycle preparations starting in earnest for 2022.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The COP26 United Nations Climate Change Conference was concluding in Glasgow, Scotland (November 1-12, 2021), with global negotiations on carbon emission targets and climate finance intensifying. North Korea had recently tested ballistic missiles (early November), escalating regional tensions in East Asia. Afghanistan remained in crisis mode under Taliban rule following the August 2021 U.S. withdrawal, with humanitarian concerns and refugee crises dominating international coverage.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The nation was settling into post-election normalcy (the 2020 election remained politically contentious with ongoing false claims of fraud, but was over 12 months old). There was a general sense of cautious optimism about pandemic recovery tempered by economic uncertainty and supply chain disruptions visible to consumers. The cultural moment was transitioning from Delta variant anxiety toward normalization of living with COVID-19; vaccination rates were the subject of intense partisan debate, and "freedom" rhetoric around mask/vaccine mandates had become deeply polarized in American discourse.
